title: papaver pilosum subsp. sparsipilosum
intro: Papaver pilosum subsp. sparsipilosum, a subspecies of the meadow poppy (Papaver rhoeas), is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Papaver pilosum subsp. sparsipilosum include its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, tinctures, or ointments, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and injuries.
